DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-04-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT APPLE INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE CODE-COMPLIANT, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES AT ISSUE WITHIN THREE WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER PER THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S INSTRUCTION. THE PARTIES SHALL SUBMIT A JOINT PROPOSED ORDER IN WORD FORMAT BY 5 P.M. ON 11/4/20. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE TO A FORM OF ORDER, THE PARTIES SHALL SUBMIT SEPARATE PROPOSED ORDERS TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM BY 5 P.M. ON 11/4/20 WITHOUT ARGUMENT. ALL DOCUMENTS SHOULD BE EMAILED TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM (PETER.CATALANOTTI@WILSONELSER.COM). JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: JENNIFER ROUX, CSR NO. 11033, EMAIL: JENROUXCSR@GMAIL.COM, TEL: 707-477-9599 (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joe Boyle (jboyle@hershlaw.com) of Hersh and Hersh for plaintiff.
Tobias Snyder (tsnyder@lewisllewellyn.com) of Lewis & Llewellyn LLP for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT APPLE INC.'s Motion For Protective Order is granted as to Ms. Kumar. Plaintiff has not met the burden of the three-prong test established in Spectra-Physics, 198 CA3d 1487, 1496 and confirmed in Carehouse Convalescent Hospital v. Superior Court (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1558. The protective order is denied as to Elena Eubank. Ms. Eubank's deposition to take place no later than April 9, 2021. Parties to meet and confer and select a date and time for Ms. Eubank's deposition. If the parties cannot agree the deposition will take place on April 9, 2021 at 9:30 via Zoom Conference.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com by March 31, 2021.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT APPLE INC.'S Motion To Compel Interrogatory Responses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Apple, Inc.'s unopposed Motion to Compel Further Responses to Interrogatories is GRANTED. All objections are overruled. Plaintiff shall serve code-compliant, verified responses to the interrogatories at issue within three weeks of notice of this order.

The parties are ordered to meet and confer per the Judge Pro Tem's instruction. The parties shall submit a joint proposed order in Word format by 5 p.m. on 11/4/20. If the parties cannot agree to a form of order, the parties shall submit separate proposed orders to the Judge Pro Tem by 5 p.m. on 11/4/20 without argument. All documents should be emailed to the Judge Pro Tem (peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com).

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Boyle, Esq. Josue Aparicio, Esq. 415-441-5544 of Hersh and Hersh for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Furman, Esq. 415-762-7692 Marc Lewis, Esq. 415-800-0591 of Lewis & Llewellyn LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues 3 points. Plaintiff addresses 2 concerns. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT APPLE INC.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Apple Inc.'s demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of the California Online Privacy Protection Act of 2003, Bus & Prof. Code 22575-22579. There is no private right of action under that Act. (People ex rel. Harris v. Delta Air Lines, Inc. (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 884, 891 ["[T]he OPPA itself does not provide for a private action or public prosecution for any violation of its provisions."].) The remainder of Apple's demurrer is overruled. (see word minutes for full details)

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Boyle, Esq. Josue Aparicio, Esq. 415-441-5544 of Hersh and Hersh for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Furman, Esq. 415-762-7692 Marc Lewis, Esq. 415-800-0591 of Lewis & Llewellyn LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues 3 points. Plaintiff addresses 2 concerns. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT APPLE INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Apple, Inc.'s motion to strike the prayer for injunctive relief is denied. A factual issue remains regarding whether awarding injunctive relief will ineluctably lead to a violation of the Stored Communications Act, as Apple contends. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to 119 and the prayer for attorney's fees pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc., 1021.5. Section 1021.5 authorizes an award of attorney's fees to successful parties only in actions that have "resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest." It is essential to such an award, among other things, that "a significant benefit, whether pecuniary or nonpecuniary, has been conferred on the general public or a large class of persons." (Code Civ. Proc. 1021.5(a).) There are no facts alleged in the amended complaint showing that these elements can be met in this case, which involves claims by one individual plaintiff.

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
